description: Business conducted that was not stated in the purpose of the called meeting.
responses: No business was conducted with this item.
It was merely an announcement. In the future, we will avoid
giving any informational updates not stated in the purpose of
the called meeting.

description: No record of annual report(s) of TE(s) laboring out of bounds.
responses: SOLA Presbytery apologizes for not
including annual reports from our TE’s laboring out of bounds
in our Minutes. It is our practice to hear reports from our
ministers and churches at the end of our meetings. At that
designated time our ministers laboring out of bounds give a
report before presbytery. We will make an effort to make note
of the reports when they are delivered by our Ministers
laboring out of bounds going forward.
No record that Presbytery corrected its
record or actions. Presbytery has not recorded its receipt of
reports from out of bounds ministers.
The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for
General 2022, noting the absence of recorded annual reports
from Teaching Elders (TEs) laboring out of bounds, as
required under BCO 8-7. We also recognize that previous
responses to similar citations were deemed unsatisfactory by
the General Assembly, and we have reviewed the Clerk's
Report to understand the rationale behind those findings.
To address this exception and rectify past
deficiencies, the presbytery has taken the following actions:
1. Identification of TEs Laboring Out of Bounds: We
compiled a comprehensive list of TEs currently laboring out
of bounds. These include: [Names Omitted].
2. Policy Implementation: To prevent future lapses,
the presbytery noted that our standing rules require all
ministers laboring out of bounds to present a report on their
work at our October stated meeting. The Stated Clerk has been
retasked with notifying TEs of this requirement annually and
ensuring the reports are presented for presbytery review.
3. Response to Prior Unsatisfactory Findings: We
understand from the Clerk's Report that prior responses lacked
specific detail regarding our actions to correct this deficiency.
The presbytery regrets these oversights and has addressed
them by ensuring that subsequent years include detailed
records of each TE's report and the presbytery's approval or
follow-up actions.
The SOLA Presbytery commits to ongoing
compliance with BCO 8-7 and requests that the General
Assembly find this response satisfactory. We are available to
provide additional documentation or clarification if needed.

description: No record of review of records of church Sessions.
responses: SOLA Presbytery apologies for failing to
note the work of our Shepherding Committee who handles the
Review of Sessional Records on behalf of SOLA Presbytery.
It is our usual practice for the Shepherding Committee to take
up the records at our Stated January meeting, review the
records, and then, hand the records back at our Stated April
Meeting. Last year the Committee was able to take up all
records and redistribute them from January to April. It was a
simple oversight on the Clerk’s part to not make note of this
in our minutes. We will attempt to do better in the future.
No record that Presbytery corrected its
record or actions. Presbytery has not recorded its review of
Session records for 2022. Presbytery is required to review the
records of lower courts annually and record in its own minutes
whether it approves, disapproves or corrects the records in any
particular ( BCO 40-1, 40-3).
The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for
General 2022, noting the absence of a recorded review of
church Session records as required under BCO 13-9.b and 401. We further recognize that previous responses to similar
citations were found unsatisfactory by the General Assembly,
and we have consulted the Clerk's Report to understand the
deficiencies identified therein.
To address this exception and resolve past
shortcomings, the presbytery has taken the following steps:
1. Review Process in 2022: SOLA Presbytery did
review presbytery records under the oversight of our
Shepherding Committee as outlined in our Standing Rules. It
was simply a failure of our clerk to note that the records were
reviewed and returned with the findings of our review
committee to the individual sessions at our April 2022
meeting.
2. Subsequent Compliance: In order to fix our
delinquency we have amended our April 2022 minutes to
reflect the report that was given to presbytery, noting that no
exceptions of substance were found by our committee in any
of the records in the minutes and were thus returned to the
sessions without need for reporting back.
3. Addressing Prior Unsatisfactory Responses: We
understand from the Clerk's Report that prior responses were
deemed unsatisfactory due to lack of specific documentation
of which Sessions were reviewed. The presbytery has
addressed this by ensuring that the minutes for subsequent
years will include detailed records of the review process.
The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
fulfilling its oversight responsibilities under BCO 13-9.b and
40-1 and respectfully requests that the General Assembly find
this response satisfactory. We stand ready to provide
additional details or documentation upon request.

description: Incomplete record of licensure exam: no statement of Christian experience and inward call.
responses: The Clerk of SOLA Presbytery apologizes
for failing to include this information in the Minutes. This is
always the first part of the floor examination that we approve
and I simply left it out. We will make every effort to include
this information in the minutes in the future.
No record that Presbytery corrected its
record. Presbytery has not recorded the receipt of a statement
of Christian experience and inward call from the licensee.
The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for its
January 22, 2022, stated meeting, noting that the record of a
licensure examination is incomplete under BCO 19-2 due to
the absence of statements regarding the candidate's Christian
experience and inward call. We also recognize that previous
responses to this citation were found unsatisfactory by the
General Assembly, and we have reviewed the Clerk's Report
to identify the deficiencies therein.
To address this exception and rectify past
shortcomings, the presbytery offers the following:
1. Details of the January 22, 2022, Examination:
Upon review, the presbytery confirmed that the Candidate
was examined for licensure at the January 22, 2022, meeting.
The examination included questions on his Christian
experience and inward call, as required by BCO 19-2, and he
provided satisfactory responses. However, these components
were not explicitly recorded in the original minutes. To
correct this, the presbytery amended the January 22, 2022,
minutes at its meeting to include: "The presbytery examined
Candidate Charles Marchman on his Christian experience,
noting his testimony of conversion, and his inward call."
2. Addressing Prior Unsatisfactory Responses: We
understand from the Clerk's Report that prior responses were
deemed unsatisfactory due to lack of an amended record. The
presbytery has addressed this by amending the 2022 minutes
with specific details and establishing a clear process for future
licensure exams.
The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
conducting and documenting licensure exams in full
compliance with BCO 19-2 and respectfully requests that the
General Assembly find this response satisfactory. We are
prepared to provide additional documentation or clarification
if needed.

description: Presbytery did not act to dissolve a TE’s call and place him on the rolls without call after his church left the denomination and he left the field.
responses: The Clerk of SOLA Presbytery apologizes
for failing to include this action in our minutes. It was an
oversight on the clerk’s part. We will make every effort to
include all actions of the Presbytery in our minutes in the
future.
No record that Presbytery corrected its
record. Presbytery has not recorded the dissolution of the TE’s
call.
The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for its
August 2, 2022, stated meeting, noting that the presbytery
failed to act under BCO 23-1 to dissolve the call of Teaching
Elder [Name Omitted] and place him on the rolls without call
after his congregation, [Church Name Omitted], withdrew
from the Presbyterian Church in America and he ceased
serving that field. We also recognize that previous responses
to this citation were deemed unsatisfactory by the General
Assembly, and we have reviewed the Clerk's Report to
understand the deficiencies identified.
To address this exception and correct past
shortcomings, the presbytery has taken the following steps:
1. Circumstances of the 2022 Omission: In June
2022, [Church Name Omitted] voted to leave the PCA, and
TE [Name Omitted] subsequently left his position as pastor of
that congregation. At the August 2, 2022, meeting, the
presbytery was informed of these events but failed to take
formal action to dissolve his call due to a lack of familiarity
with the process to handle these sorts of cases. The minutes of
that meeting do not reflect the required action under BCO 231. We apologize for this failure.
2. Corrective Action: At our April 2025 stated
meeting, the presbytery will address this omission by voting
to dissolve TE [Name Omitted]'s call to [Church Name
Omitted], effective July 3, 2022. This action will be recorded
in the minutes at our April 2025 stated meeting
3. Verification of Status: TE [Name Omitted]
received a call to serve as the minister at [Church Name
Omitted], Louisiana and remains a Minister in Good Standing
in our presbytery. WE recognize the formal error of approving
this call without first dissolving TE [Name Omitted]'s call
with [Church Name Omitted] and we will not make the same
mistake again.
4. Procedural Safeguards: To prevent future lapses
under BCO 23-1, the presbytery will make sure to
appropriately label TEs according to their status in their
church. In addition, we will revise our standing rules to clarify
which committee is responsible for overseeing the dissolution
of calls.
The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
upholding BCO 23-1 and respectfully requests that the
General Assembly find this response satisfactory. We are
available to provide additional documentation or clarification
if requested.

description: Presbytery organized a church that had not elected ruling elders or called a pastor. [attached Dec. 19, 2022 commission minutes].
responses: The commission minutes unfortunately
were incomplete and we apologize for not completing them
prior to filing our RPR report last year. Five men were duly
elected and installed as Ruling Elders and three men were duly
elected and installed as Deacons by the church. Please forgive
our failure to include that information. We will make every
effort to correct this failure in the future.
Presbytery’s response does not record the
names of officer candidates and all necessary steps for officer
election and examination.
The SOLA Presbytery acknowledges the
exception of substance cited by the Committee on Review of
Presbytery Records (RPR) for its December 19, 2022
commission meeting to organize [Church Name Omitted] in
St. Francisville, Louisiana as a particular church under BCO
5-9 without evidence of elected ruling elders or a called
pastor. We also recognize that previous responses to this
citation were deemed unsatisfactory by the General
Assembly, and we have reviewed the Clerk's Report to
address the deficiencies identified therein.
To resolve this exception and correct past
shortcomings, the presbytery provides the following:
1. Circumstances of the October 22, 2022,
Organization: At the October 22, 2022, meeting, the
presbytery voted to organize [Church Name Omitted] as a
particular church. At that time, the congregation had not yet
elected ruling elders or called a pastor due and was going to
be doing so at a called meeting prior to the Commission's
meeting on December 19th.
2. Corrective Action via Commission: On December
19, 2022, a presbytery commission met fora particularization
service. The commission oversaw the election of 5 Ruling
Elders ([Names Omitted]) and facilitated the call of TE [Name
Omitted] as pastor, with the congregation voting to approve
both actions on that date. The commission's report, adopted at
the January 28, 2023 stated presbytery meeting has been
amended to reflect this action taken by the commission of
SOLA Presbytery.
3. Addressing Prior Unsatisfactory Responses: We
understand from the Clerk's Report that prior responses were
unsatisfactory due to a lack of detail on corrective actions. The
presbytery has addressed this by providing specific actions
taken on December 19, 2022, amending the 2022 minutes, and
establishing a preventive process.
The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
organizing churches in accordance with BCO 5-9 and
respectfully requests that the General Assembly find this
response satisfactory and we are available to supply additional
documentation if needed.

description: Presbytery installed a pastor without having received or approved a call.
responses: The commission failed to note that the TE
did receive a call and it was placed in his hands. Please forgive
this failure on our part. We will make every effort to include
this information going forward.
No record that Presbytery corrected its
actions ( RAO 16-10.b.1). Presbytery has not approved the
terms of call for the TE.
The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for its
October 22, 2022, stated meeting, noting that the presbytery
installed Teaching Elder TE [Name Omitted] as pastor of
[Church Name Omitted] Church without having received or
approved a call, in violation of BCO 21-9. We also recognize
that previous responses to this citation were deemed
unsatisfactory by the General Assembly, and we have
reviewed the Clerk's Report to address the deficiencies
identified.
To resolve this exception and correct past
shortcomings, the presbytery offers the following:
1. Circumstances: At the October 22, 2022, meeting,
it is noted that SOLA Presbytery approved the petition of
[Church Name Omitted] church to be particularized as a
congregation of the PCA. The Presbytery voted to receive
[Church Name Omitted] and a commission was appointed by
the Moderator for the threefold purpose of particularizing
[Church Name Omitted] Church, approving the call of TE
[Name Omitted] and examining the Ruling Elders to be
elected by the congregation. There was a discussion at that
time about the irregularity of approving the particularization
of [Church Name Omitted] without the pastoral call being
approved by Presbytery.
2. Corrective Action: At that time we understood that
since TE [Name Omitted] had already been approved to
organize the church plant and since he was already a member
in good standing of our presbytery that the irregularity of the
request could be rectified prior to the commission meeting on
December 18, 2022. Therefore, prior to the December 18,
2022 commission meeting, [Church Name Omitted] voted to
call TE [Name Omitted] as their pastor. At that meeting the
commission of SOLA Presbytery reviewed this call and
approved it as part of its actions on behalf of presbytery,
however, this action was not reflected in the minutes
presented to Presbytery. Therefore, we have corrected the
minutes to include the commission's approval of TE's Shaw's
call to [Church Name Omitted] Church.
3. Verification: The presbytery confirmed with
[Church Name Omitted] that the congregation indeed voted to
call TE [Name Omitted] on December 19, 2025, and TE
[Name Omitted] accepted the call. This supports the
retroactive approval and ensures compliance with BCO 21-9.
4. Addressing Prior Unsatisfactory Responses: We
understand from the Clerk's Report that prior responses were
unsatisfactory due to "failure to document the call's approval"
and "lack of corrective action". The presbytery has addressed
this by retroactively approving the call, amending the
minutes, and establishing a preventive process.
The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
adhering to BCO 21-9 and respectfully requests that the
General Assembly find this response satisfactory. We are
prepared to provide additional documentation or clarification
if needed.

description: No record of review of records of church Sessions.
responses: The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for
General 2023, noting the absence of a recorded review of
church Session records as required under BCO 13-9.b and 401. We further recognize that previous responses to similar
citations were found unsatisfactory by the General Assembly,
and we have consulted the Clerk's Report to understand the
deficiencies identified therein.
To address this exception and resolve past
shortcomings, the presbytery has taken the following steps:
1. Review Process in 2023 (Same as the process in
2022): SOLA Presbytery did review presbytery records under
the oversight of our Shepherding Committee as outlined in our
Standing Rules. It was simply a failure of our clerk to note
that the records were reviewed and returned with the findings
of our review committee to the individual sessions at our April
2022 meeting.
2. Subsequent Compliance: In order to fix our
delinquency we have amended our April 2023 minutes to
reflect the report that was given to presbytery, noting that no
exceptions of substance were found by our committee in any
of the records in the minutes and were thus returned to the
sessions without need for reporting back.
3. Addressing Prior Unsatisfactory Responses: We
understand from the Clerk's Report that prior responses were
deemed unsatisfactory due to lack of specific documentation
of which Sessions were reviewed. The presbytery has
addressed this by ensuring that the 2023 (and subsequent
years) include detailed records of the review process.
The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
fulfilling its oversight responsibilities under BCO 13-9.b and
40-1 and respectfully requests that the General Assembly find
this response satisfactory. We stand ready to provide
additional details or documentation upon request.

description: No record of annual report(s) of TE(s) laboring out of bounds.
responses: The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for
General 2023, noting that the presbytery's minutes for 2023
do not include annual reports from candidates under care, as
required by BCO 18-6.
To address this exception, the presbytery provides the
following:
1. Circumstances of the 2023 Omission: The
presbytery currently has two candidates under care: Candidate
[Name Omitted] and Candidate [Name Omitted]. Upon
review by consultation with the chair of the Candidates
Committee these candidates submitted annual reports to the
Candidates Committee throughout the year and the
Candidates committee reviews those reports and reports on
their review at the October presbytery meeting. These actions
were not recorded in our minutes due to a clerical oversight
by our Clerk.
2. Corrective Action: the commission of presbytery
assigned to deal with the exceptions of substance have
amended our minutes to reflect this report delivered at our
October 2023 stated Presbytery meeting.
The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
overseeing candidates under care per BCO 18-6 and
respectfully requests that the General Assembly find this
response satisfactory. We are prepared to provide copies of
the 2023 candidate reports or additional documentation if
needed.

description: Annual reports of candidates not included.
responses: The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for its
October 28, 2023, stated meeting (p. 42), noting that the
minutes lack a record of the ordination questions from BCO
21-5 being administered to Teaching Elder [Name Omitted]
during his ordination service.
To address this exception, the presbytery provides the
following:
1. Circumstances of the November 12, 2023,
Ordination: On November 12, 2023, TE [Name Omitted] was
ordained as a teaching elder during a church service held at
[Church Name Omitted], in accordance with BCO 21-5. The
presbytery's commission, appointed at the October 28, 2023
meeting, conducted the service, and the ordination questions
from BCO 21-5 were administered to TE [Name Omitted],
who answered them affirmatively, but the minutes of that
commission meeting incorrectly say that the questions from
BCO 21-9 were administered.
2. Corrective Action: The commission of SOLA
Presbytery empowered to address this deficiency has
confirmed with the commission members that during the
November 12, 2023 service, TE [Name Omitted] was asked
the ordination questions from BCO 21-5, including his
affirmation of PCA doctrine, discipline, and ministerial
duties, and he responded affirmatively. The commission has
amended the minutes of their commission to reflect this
correction by adding: "During the ordination service for TE
[Name Omitted] at [Church Name Omitted] on November 12,
2023 the commission administered the ordination questions
per BCO 21-5, which he answered in the affirmative."
The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
conducting ordinations in accordance with BCO 21-5, within
the context of a church service, and respectfully requests that
the General Assembly find this response satisfactory. We are
prepared to provide additional documentation, such as the
commission's report or service order, if needed.

description: No record of sixmonths’ membership or endorsement by candidate’s Session.
responses: The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for its
October 28, 2023, stated meeting (p. 33), noting that the
minutes lack a record of Mr. [Name Omitted]'s six-months'
communicant membership in a PCA church or endorsement
by his Session, as required by BCO 18-2, prior to his being
taken under care as a candidate for the gospel ministry.
1. Circumstances: At the October 28, 2023, stated
meeting, the presbytery voted to receive Mr. [Name Omitted]
as a candidate under care. As reported by our candidates
committee, it was determined that Mr. [Name Omitted] had
not been a communicant member of [Church Name Omitted]
at least six-months prior to October 28, 2023. The Presbytery
acted, upon the recommendation of the Candidates
Committee to approve Mr. [Name Omitted] as a candidate for
gospel ministry given his previous labors in the ministry and
his Session's endorsement prior to October 2023. These facts
were inadvertently omitted from the minutes due to clerical
oversight.
2. Corrective Action: The commission of SOLA
Presbytery assigned to respond to these exceptions has
confirmed with the Candidates Committee of Presbytery and
[Church Name Omitted] that Mr. [Name Omitted] was
endorsed by [Church Name Omitted]'s session and approved
by Presbytery under the exceptional provision of BCO 18-2.
Further we have corrected our minutes of the October 28,
2023 stated meeting to reflect this action.
The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
adhering to BCO 18-2 and respectfully requests that the
General Assembly find this response satisfactory. We are
prepared to provide additional documentation, including
Session records, if needed.

description: No record of the reasons why Presbytery considers an out-of-bounds work to be a valid Christian ministry.
responses: The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for its
October 28, 2023, stated meeting (p. 34), noting that the
minutes lack a record of the reasons why the presbytery
considered Teaching Elder [Name Omitted]'s out-of-bounds
work a valid Christian ministry under BCO 20-1.
To address this exception, the presbytery provides the
following:
1. Circumstances of the October 28, 2023, Action: At
the October 28, 2023, meeting, the presbytery approved a call
for TE [Name Omitted] to serve in an out-of-bounds work as
Executive Director of Reformed Youth Ministries. The
presbytery evaluated the work and deemed it a valid Christian
ministry based on its alignment with the gospel priorities of
the PCA, its founding by PCA Teaching Elders and Members,
and its close collaboration with the PCA MNA Permanent
Committee. However, due to the assumption that approval
implied validity, these reasons were not explicitly recorded in
the minutes.
2. Corrective Action: The Commission of presbytery
amended the October 28, 2023, minutes to include the
rationale for approving TE [Name Omitted]'s call: "The
presbytery considers TE [Name Omitted]'s work at Reformed
Youth Ministries a valid Christian ministry because it
advances the same mission of the PCA and aligns with the
PCA's mission and vision." This amendment is recorded in
Minutes.
The SOLA Presbytery affirms its commitment to
properly approving and documenting calls under BCO 20-1
and respectfully requests that the General Assembly find this
response satisfactory. We are prepared to provide additional
documentation, such as TE [Name Omitted]'s report or
organizational materials, if needed.

description: No record of the ordination questions being given to a candidate during his ordination service.
responses: The Southern Louisiana Presbytery
acknowledges the exception of substance cited by the
Committee on Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) for
General 2023, noting the absence of recorded annual reports
from Teaching Elders laboring out of bounds, as required
under BCO 8-7. We also recognize that previous responses to
similar citations were deemed unsatisfactory by the General
Assembly, and we have reviewed the Clerk's Report to
understand the rationale behind those findings.
To address this exception and rectify past
deficiencies, the presbytery has taken the following actions:
1. Identification of TEs Laboring Out of Bounds: We
compiled a comprehensive list of TEs currently laboring out
of bounds. These include: [Names Omitted].
2. Policy Implementation: To prevent future lapses,
the presbytery noted that our standing rules require all
ministers laboring out of bounds to present a report on their
work at our October stated meeting. The Stated Clerk is tasked
with notifying TEs of this requirement annually and ensuring
the reports are presented for presbytery review.
3. Response to Prior Unsatisfactory Findings: We
understand from the Clerk's Report that prior responses lacked
specific detail or lack of evidence of presbytery action. The
presbytery regrets these oversights and has addressed them by
ensuring the 2023 minutes (and subsequent years) include
detailed records of each TE's report and the presbytery's
approval or follow-up actions.
The SOLA Presbytery commits to ongoing
compliance with BCO 8-7 and requests that the General
Assembly find this response satisfactory. We are available to
provide additional documentation or clarification if needed.

not believe that it erred in passing the motion.

description: Candidate’s stated difference(s) not recorded. Response:Response: The candidate coming for ordination had been licensed at the previous Stated Meeting of Presbytery where his stated differences were adjudged “as more than semantic, but not striking at the vitals of the system of doctrine as set forth in the Westminster Confession of Faith and Catechisms.” A note was added to the February minutes that the candidate’s stated differences had not changed since he was licensed at the September 13, 2022 Stated Meeting. 1. WSC 60 states that on the Sabbath day we are to observe “a holy resting all that day, even from such worldly employments and recreations as are lawful on other days; and spending the whole time in the public and private exercises of God’s worship.” While I largely agree with the Westminster view of the Sabbath, I believe that lawful, restful recreational activities are permissible on the Sabbath day. 2. WLC 109, in discussing the Second Commandment, describes this command as forbidding “the making any representation of God, of all or of any of the three persons, either inwardly in our mind, or outwardly in any kind of image or likeness of any creature whatsoever.” I believe that the formation of certain images of the three persons of the Trinity in one’s mind is almost impossible to avoid, particularly as Jesus took on flesh as a human person, and the Holy Spirit is depicted variously as a dove, tongues of fire, etc. I don’t find images such as these to be particularly harmful in things like children’s books, granted that these images are not to be worshiped nor are they to be perceived as actual portrayals of the persons of the godhead.

own confession of the sin of cheating on your ordination
exams, we, the Tennessee Valley Presbytery, in the name and
by the authority of the Lord Jesus Christ, do admonish you for
this violation of our Lord’s commands. We warn you of the
severity of this sin, and we encourage you to take all necessary
steps of restitution.We further exhort you to examine your
own heart and to consider how similar sin may be avoided in
the future. We encourage you to remember that God is faithful
and just to forgive all who confess and that you are counted
as righteous in the court of heaven through faith in Jesus
Christ alone, who loved you and gave himself up for you. May
God bring you restoration, peace, and strength as you continue
to labor in the future as a minister of Jesus Christ.

No record of notice of called meeting with 10-day notice in the record.
No record that members of interim/provisional Session were called by the congregation.
No record regarding the reception of a certificate of dismission from transferring presbytery (May be accomplished through Clerks portal).
Annual reports of candidates not included.
responses: Warrior Presbytery apologizes for this
oversight and will ensure all candidates in our presbytery
make an annual report.

description: The Moderator may be elected for at most 1 year at a time. The Standing Rules state that he "shall serve until his successor is elected", which has no limit.
responses: Warrior Presbytery apologizes for this
oversight and has begun the process of revising our Standing
Rules to explicitly limit the moderator to, at most, one year of
service at a time.

description: No record that TE laboring as an interim in an EPC church will have the full freedom to maintain and teach the full doctrine of our church.
No record of which presbytery TE is transferring from and whether he is a member in good standing.
responses: As far as Presbytery can discern, this is an
error from the Committee on Review of Presbytery Records.
Presbytery did not meet on April 26, 2022.
Although the Presbytery did not meet on
April 26, 2022, a commission of presbytery did meet on that
date to examine a TE for transfer (page 2022-20). The
commission minutes fail to record which presbytery the TE is
transferring from and whether he is a member in good
standing.
While Presbytery regrets being unable to
locate said minutes last year and is thankful for RPR's help
finding them, Presbytery respectfully disagrees with the
exception. The cited minutes do record the Presbytery of the
Presbyterian Church in Brazil from which the candidate came.
Moreover, BCO 13-6 does not require recording the standing
of a candidate in the minutes.

description: Reports on every intern by the committee charged with the oversight of interns at each stated meeting not included.
responses: Presbytery agrees with this exception,
though it notes progress has been made from prior years.
Presbytery intends to continue making progress on accurately
reflecting in its minutes its oversight of interns.

description: Annual reports of candidates not included.
responses: Presbytery agrees with the exception and
promises to do better in the future.

description: Annual report of Interns not included.
responses: Presbytery agrees with the exception and
promises to do better in the future.

description: 3/4 vote for waiving internship requirement not recorded.
responses: Presbytery agrees with the exception. The
record has been corrected to indicate that the approval of the
candidate's prior experience passed without objection.

description: No record of sixmonths membership of candidate.
No record that Presbytery made determinations required by BCO 38-3 (specifically in applying a. or b.).
responses: The Presbytery agrees with the exception,
has voted to correct its minutes from April 22, 2023, to reflect
that TE [Name Omitted] was removed from the presbytery
rolls according to BCO 38-3(a) since he was in good standing,
but that his removal to and new membership in the Anglican
Church of North America is an irregularity. The Presbytery
promises to be more careful in the future.

description: No record of sixmonths membership for candidates.
responses: The Presbytery agrees with the exception,
has voted to correct its minutes from April 22, 2023, to reflect
that [Name Omitted] had been a member of [Church Name
Omitted] since July 2022 and [Name Omitted] had been a
member of [Church Name Omitted] for six years. The
Presbytery promises to be more careful in the future.

description: No record of sixmonths membership for candidates.
responses: The Presbytery agrees with the exception,
has voted to correct its minutes from July 22, 2023, to reflect
that [Name Omitted] had been a member of [Church Name
Ommitted] for seven months. The Presbytery promises to be
more careful in the future.
